sending.title: Outbound sending
sending.subtitle: Receiving stays on this server. Choose how outbound mail leaves it.
sending.modeDirectDesc: This server delivers outbound mail itself. Simple, but fresh-VPS IPs are often blocklisted and many hosts block outbound port 25 — mail may land in spam or fail.
sending.modeRelayDesc: Hand outbound mail to a trusted relay (Amazon SES or any SMTP relay). Recommended — best deliverability. Your inbox and all receiving DNS stay exactly as they are.
sending.scopeAllHint: Every domain on this server sends via the relay.
sending.scopeSelectedHint: Only the chosen domains relay; the rest deliver direct. Others need their own SES identity.
sending.noDomains: No domains found on this server yet.
sending.passwordKeep: Leave blank to keep the current password
sending.mailFromHint: Optional. A subdomain like bounce.example.com for SPF/bounce alignment. Configure it in SES too.
sending.identitiesHint: SES verifies each sending domain separately. Paste each relayed domain's DKIM records (optional — amavis already signs, so DMARC aligns without them, but SES needs the domain verified to send).
sending.dkimHint: Paste the 3 CNAME records SES shows you (SES console → Verified identities → your domain → DKIM). We render + verify them on the DNS tab.
sending.guidanceVerify: Verify your domain in the Amazon SES console.
sending.guidanceSandbox: Request production access — SES starts in a sandbox that only sends to verified addresses.
sending.guidanceCreds: Use SES SMTP credentials (an IAM SMTP user) — not your AWS access keys.
sending.guidanceDns: Publish the DNS records on the DNS tab, then re-scan to confirm.
sending.disabled: Relay disabled — back to direct delivery.
sending.dnsHint: SES send-hop records now appear on the DNS tab. Publish + verify them for full deliverability.

overview.setupGuidesDesc: Step-by-step walkthroughs for every common way to use this mailbox - from your phone to your codebase. Opens in a new tab.

guides.iosSubtitle: Add as IMAP on iPhone / iPad / Mac
guides.androidSubtitle: Add as a third-party IMAP account

domains.heading: Domains
domains.description: Each row maps to a row in vmail.domain on the mail VPS. Additional domains accept mail once their MX record points here.
domains.pendingDescAfter: once these are live. The MX record points back to your existing mail server; DKIM was generated automatically when the domain was added. Add them at your DNS provider, then click
domains.emptyDesc: Add a domain to start hosting mailboxes under it. Make sure its MX record points to this mail server.

create.title: Add domain
create.description: Adds a new entry to vmail.domain on the mail VPS. Make sure the MX record for this domain points to the mail server before sending mail through it.
create.domainHint: e.g. acme.com - no protocol, no path.
create.descHint: Optional - shown only in the dashboard.
create.quotaHint: Optional. Default for new mailboxes in this domain; can be overridden per mailbox. Leave blank for unlimited.

editForm.title: Edit {domain}
editForm.activeDesc: When unchecked, mail addressed to this domain is rejected at the MX gate.

deleteForm.title: Delete {domain}?
deleteForm.descDependents: This domain still has {parts}. Tick the box below to delete them too - mailbox files on disk will be removed and cannot be undone.
deleteForm.descSimple: Removes the domain row and any admin mappings. Mail to this domain will start being rejected immediately.
deleteForm.alsoDeleteDesc: Permanently removes every mailbox under this domain (DB rows and Maildir files on disk) along with all aliases. This cannot be reversed.
deleteForm.errorDependents: Domain still has {mailboxes} mailbox(es) and {aliases} alias(es). Tick "Also delete…" to remove them, or delete them manually first.

dns.heading: DNS records
dns.description: The records each domain needs at your DNS provider for mail delivery. Pick a domain to view its records and verify they're published.
dns.recordsDesc: Publish these at your DNS provider for {domain}.
dns.recordsEmpty: The install wizard generates records during the DKIM step; additional domains get theirs when added.
dns.noRecordsFor: No DNS records on file for {domain}.
dns.checkDesc: Live public-DNS lookup for {domain}, compared to the records above.
dns.runLookupBefore: Run a live DNS lookup for
dns.runLookupAfter: and compare it to the records above.
dns.noRecordsVerify: No records on file for {domain} to verify.

mailboxes.heading: Mailboxes
mailboxes.description: Each row is an IMAP/SMTP account on the mail server. Passwords are hashed with doveadm SSHA512 before storage.
mailboxes.emptyDesc: Create the first mailbox for {domain}. Passwords are hashed with doveadm SSHA512 - only the hash is stored on disk.

create.title: New mailbox · {domain}
create.description: Creates an IMAP/SMTP account on the mail server. Password is hashed with doveadm SSHA512 on the VPS - only the hash is stored.
create.fullNameHint: Display name shown in mail clients. Optional.
create.passwordHint: At least 8 characters. Or generate one.

editForm.title: Edit {username}
editForm.newPasswordHint: Leave blank to keep the current one. At least 8 characters if set.
editForm.activeDesc: When unchecked, IMAP login fails and incoming mail bounces.

deleteForm.title: Delete {username}?
deleteForm.descHard: Hard delete: removes the DB rows AND the Maildir on disk. Cannot be undone.
deleteForm.descSoft: Soft delete: sets active = 0 and inserts a row in vmail.deleted_mailboxes. The mail server's cleanup cron picks it up later. The Maildir stays on disk until then.
deleteForm.hardDeleteDesc: Remove DB rows and Maildir files immediately. Cannot be undone.
deleteForm.postmasterNote: The postmaster mailbox is hard-delete protected - only soft delete is available, so the mail server's bootstrap account stays intact.

health.heading: Health
health.description: Live daemon status + DNS scan. Daemons refresh on a 10-second poll; DNS scan runs once on open and again whenever you click Rescan.
health.daemonsDesc: Mail-server processes managed by systemd on the VPS.
health.dnsScanDescFor: Live public-DNS check of every record we expect for {domain}.
health.dnsScanDesc: Live public-DNS check of every record this server expects.
health.dnsEmpty: No DNS records on file yet. Finish the install wizard to populate the scan.

summary.allGoodSub: Daemons are running and every DNS record is published correctly.
summary.almostSubOne: {count} optional DNS record could be improved - see DNS scan below.
summary.almostSubOther: {count} optional DNS records could be improved - see DNS scan below.
summary.notInstalledSubOne: Mail still works without it; install only if you actually need this component.
summary.notInstalledSubOther: Mail still works without them; install only if you actually need these components.

backup.heading: Backup
backup.description: Snapshot this mail server to one of your backup destinations. To move a server, back it up here, set the target up with the install wizard, then restore this backup onto it.
backup.whatDesc: Accounts and auth are always included; message data and keys are optional.
backup.accountsDesc: Mailboxes (with password hashes), domains, and aliases — the vmail database.
backup.messageDesc: Every stored email (the /var/vmail maildirs). Can be large.
backup.keysDesc: DKIM keys + config + mail-state.json. Keeps DKIM valid on restore — but the archive then holds private keys and secrets.
backup.keysWarning: The backup will contain password hashes, DKIM private keys, and plaintext secrets. Keep the destination private.
backup.destDesc: Backups stream to a destination you manage on the Backups page.
backup.noDest: No backup destinations yet. Add one (e.g. an existing server) on the Backups page.
backup.started: Backup started. It'll appear below as it runs.

advanced.resetDesc: Wipes openship's tracking of this install: the on-server state file (/root/.openship/mail-state.json) AND the mail-server registry row. The running mail stack is not touched - every mailbox, message, and queue stays intact - but you'll need to re-run the setup wizard to manage it again (Remove keeps the state file so you can just re-adopt).
advanced.resetBox: After this, the /emails page will show the install wizard again for this server. You can then either re-run from step 1 or pick up from a specific step.
advanced.forgetDesc: Removes this server from openship's mail list only. The mail stack keeps running and nothing is uninstalled - no mailboxes, messages, or DNS are touched. Re-add it anytime with Scan for an existing install.
advanced.forgetBox: Use this to clear a stale or mismarked entry. Because the on-server install is left intact, re-adopting it later restores full management with no reinstall.
advanced.protocolDesc: Host, port, and encryption for inbound (IMAP) and outbound (SMTP). Use these when configuring a mail client by hand - most clients can also discover them from the email address alone.

protocolNote.p1: Username on both servers is your
protocolNote.p4: ; openship never stores the cleartext, so use a manager (1Password, Bitwarden, etc.) to save it.

advanced.dangerDesc: These actions can disrupt a working mail server or clear important tracking state. Read the description on each card before clicking.
advanced.rerunDesc: Opens the install wizard pointed at this server. Useful after a DNS change, a domain rename, or to retry a failed step. The wizard detects an existing install and offers per-step retry rather than wiping state.
advanced.resetCardDesc: Removes openship's tracking of this install - the on-server state file (/root/.openship/mail-state.json) and the registry row. Does NOT uninstall the mail stack or touch any mailboxes; the server keeps running, but you'll re-run the setup wizard to manage it again. Use after a manual purge or re-image when tracking has drifted from reality.
advanced.removeDesc: Stops managing this server - drops it from the /emails list only. The mail stack keeps running and the on-server state file is left intact, so you can re-adopt it later via Scan for an existing install. Use to clear a stale or mismarked entry.
advanced.toolsDesc: Recovery actions that touch the running stack without altering state. Safe to run anytime; mail flow blips for a few seconds.
advanced.restartCardDesc: Cycles every mail daemon at once. The fastest fix when something flaked after a deploy - login fails, queue stalls, etc.
advanced.restartConfirmDesc: Cycles every running mail daemon (the queue receiver, the IMAP service, the spam pipeline, fail2ban, and PostgreSQL). Mail flow pauses for a few seconds while units come back up. Mailboxes, queues, and DNS are untouched.
advanced.restartBox: Use this when something looks off right after a deploy or a config change - most transient breakage clears with a cycle.

test.title: Test sending
test.description: Send a real test email through this mail server to confirm delivery, DKIM, and SPF.

reputation.body: Brand-new sending domains have no reputation yet, so providers may file early mail to spam for the first 24-48 hours. This is normal. Mark messages as "not spam" from your testing inbox and deliverability improves quickly.

welcome.liveDescAfter: is accepting mail. Send yourself a test to confirm it.
welcome.sentAfter: . Should arrive within a minute - check your inbox and your spam folder.

restore.noTargetsBefore: No other installed mail servers. Set the target up with the install wizard first (same domain,
restore.warnMain: This wipes the {which} server's accounts, domains and aliases, then loads the backup.
restore.warnForkExtra: The target should be set up with the same domain. After migrating, repoint MX/A DNS + PTR to the new server.
restore.subRunning: Moving accounts, domains and message data. This can take a while.

sendTest.sentToast: Test email sent. Check inbox + spam.
sendTest.title: Send a test email
sendTest.sendsFromAfter: — the mailbox is provisioned on demand for this domain and DKIM-signed with the domain's key.
sendTest.fromHint: Pick the domain to authenticate as. Useful for verifying a newly-added domain after its DNS records have propagated.

shared.noJournal: No journal entries for this unit.
